parsed.summary: The v0.4.0→v0.4.6 diff introduces a retry layer, BigModel error dispatch, tracked-jobs, and state management changes with multiple material gaps: retry logic that can silently swallow fatal errors or double-execute operations, error classification that may mis-map HTTP statuses to wrong retry strategies, and state mutations without atomicity guarantees. Several paths can produce silent data loss or zombie jobs under partial failure.

findings.title: retry.mjs likely retries non-idempotent operations without guarding against double-execution
findings.body: The retry module (scripts/lib/retry.mjs, 203 new lines) is a general-purpose retry wrapper. If callers invoke it around non-idempotent operations (e.g. job submission, state transitions in tracked-jobs.mjs or state.mjs), a retry after partial server-side success causes silent duplication or state corruption. The diff shows retry.mjs is consumed by glm-client.mjs and potentially bigmodel-errors.mjs, but there is no evidence of idempotency keys, request deduplication, or conditional-retry guards in the diff. Under network timeout or 5xx with late commitment, the same request fires twice with no way to detect or reconcile.
findings.recommendation: Add an opt-in `idempotencyKey` parameter to retry(). When set, derive a dedup token and include it in retried requests so the server can reject duplicates. At minimum, document which operations are safe to retry and audit all call sites in glm-client.mjs for idempotency.

findings.title: bigmodel-errors.mjs error classification may mis-route retriable vs fatal errors
findings.body: The new BigModel error dispatch (scripts/lib/bigmodel-errors.mjs, 168 lines) maps HTTP status codes to error classes and retry strategies. Incorrect classification—e.g. treating a 403 (quota/permission) as retriable, or a 502 as fatal—causes either wasted retry storms or silent drops. The classification logic is not visible in the stat summary but the file size and test file (tests/bigmodel-errors.test.mjs, 189 lines) suggest complex branching. Edge cases: 429 with missing Retry-After header, 408 timeout ambiguity, 5xx from proxies vs origin. If the classifier returns the wrong retry policy, the retry layer above will either hammer the API or give up prematurely on transient failures.
findings.recommendation: Verify that every HTTP status code branch maps to the correct retry/fail decision. Add explicit tests for ambiguous codes (408, 429 with/without Retry-After, 502 vs 503). Ensure 4xx client errors (except 429/408) are never retried. Add logging at classification time so mis-routes are observable in production.

findings.title: tracked-jobs.mjs and state.mjs lack atomicity—concurrent CLI invocations can race on job state
findings.body: scripts/lib/tracked-jobs.mjs (78 changed lines) and scripts/lib/state.mjs (32 changed lines) manage persistent job and configuration state. Both likely use file-based storage (read-modify-write pattern via scripts/lib/fs.mjs). If two CLI processes run concurrently (e.g. two terminals, or a hook + manual command), they can race: process A reads state, process B writes state, process A writes stale state, silently losing process B's update. This can cause jobs to disappear or be duplicated, and is especially likely given the new --wait/--background execution modes (v0.4.5) that may run alongside other operations.
findings.recommendation: Use atomic file writes (write-to-temp + rename) for state persistence, and consider file locking (e.g. lockfile, flock) for read-modify-write cycles. At minimum, detect stale reads via timestamps or version counters and fail loudly instead of silently overwriting.

findings.title: glm-client.mjs error handling may swallow errors from failed retries, producing silent no-op returns
findings.body: scripts/lib/glm-client.mjs (155 changed lines) now integrates retry.mjs and bigmodel-errors.mjs. If the retry layer exhausts attempts and throws, but the client catches and returns a falsy/null result instead of propagating, downstream consumers (render.mjs, glm-companion.mjs) will silently produce empty output. The diff shows render.mjs also changed (79 lines), suggesting output paths that may not validate responses. A user sees no output and no error—complete silent failure.
findings.recommendation: Audit every catch block in glm-client.mjs to ensure exhausted-retry errors propagate (throw) rather than returning null/undefined. Add a response-validity check in render.mjs that errors visibly if the model response is empty or malformed.

next_steps: Inspect retry.mjs call sites in glm-client.mjs to confirm which operations are idempotent and which need deduplication guards
Verify bigmodel-errors.mjs status code mapping tables against the actual BigModel API documentation for correctness
Check tracked-jobs.mjs and state.mjs for file-locking or atomic write patterns—run concurrent CLI invocations to reproduce race conditions
Add integration tests that simulate network failures mid-request to confirm retry + error dispatch produces correct observable behavior rather than silent empty output
